_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for normalizeAgentId parity. The controller owns the authoritative normalizeAgentId (openclaw-agent-config.ts) and apps/web re-declares it (lib/kiloclaw/agent-id.ts) because the architecture wall blocks importing controller code into the web app. BOTH test suites load THIS file and assert their implementation produces `expected` for each `input` (controller: openclaw-agent-config.test.ts; web: lib/kiloclaw/agent-id.test.ts). Because the corpus is shared, changing either implementation's output for a listed input fails that side, and changing the rule means updating this file AND both implementations or the other side fails. Keep it as a pure input->expected list.
